methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Neustark is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Valentin Gutknecht and Johannes Tiefenthaler, having raised $70M to date. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private) in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $70M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $133M. The peer-median method references six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $355M (range: $120M–$5.0B). The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in the climate tech sector; Commonwealth Fusion Systems trades at $5.0B while the lower quartile sits at $120M.

Triangulation across these two methods produces a valuation range of $113M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $244M. The $113M floor reflects a conservative stage-progression scenario, while the $408M ceiling approaches the peer-median result. The $355M peer median itself sits in the upper half of the range, suggesting material sensitivity to peer selection and scaling metrics. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the extreme peer outliers (particularly Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B) and whether Neustark's capital efficiency and market traction align with the median or lower-quartile peer cohort.
